<a id="_Hlk221786341"></a>__How to use the Secure Adaptable Logger API functions in C\-Program application__

The Secure Adaptable Logger \(SAL\) is incorporated into the user application when compiled with the LoggerLibClient\.a library\. The SAL operations validate and securely protect application log messages\.

The following three API functions:

- LoggerStartUp\(\) initializes internal structures and starts the logger capabilities\.
- <a id="_Hlk221786518"></a>LoggerAppsMsg\(\) generates application log messages\.
- LoggerShutDown\(\) frees\-up internal structures and stops the logger capabilities\.

Are shown in the following example C\-Program:

<a id="_Hlk221978176"></a>\#include <stdio\.h>

main\(\)

\{

    LoggerStartUp\(\);

	

       …application code…

    <a id="_Hlk223618194"></a>LoggerAppsMsg\(1,100,3,"Message that generates 1 string and 2 integer arguments \[%s\], \[%d\] and \[%d\]","String Argument", 98,99\);

      …application code…

   LoggerShutDown\(\);

\}<a id="_Hlk221531119"></a>

<a id="_Hlk221978396"></a>Which generates the following formatted output log message:

<a id="_Hlk221978546"></a><a id="_Hlk221980233"></a>02/09/2026 12:01:55 <a id="_Hlk221978609"></a>\[3650:sample\_program\] \[DATABASE\] \[INFORMATIONAL\] Message that generates 1 string and 2 integer arguments \[String Argument\], \[98\] and \[99\]

Where:

- “02/09/2026 12:01:55” is the date and timestamp when the log message was generated\.
- “\[3650:sample\_program\]” is the user application process id and name\.
- “\[DATABASE\]” is the message category grouping for related application log messages\.
- “\[INFORMATIONAL\]” is the severity for the log message\.
- The remainder is derived from the LoggerAppsMsg\(\) call which is the C\-Programming Language printf\(\) formatted style string specifies and data values\.

__API function execution flow:__

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Refer to LoggerAppsMsg\(\) manual page for the argument details\.

Refer to LoggerMessageDefines\.txt document about the LoggerAppsMsg\(\) arguments 1, 100 and 3 are used and converted to the formatted output log message\.
